name: Peanut Butterfinger Chunk Cookies
recipeInstructions:
NOTE: Do not use freshly ground or "old-fashioned style" peanut butter.
Butterfinger bars should be cut into 1/2-inch pieces.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Lightly grease 2 cookie sheets.
1. Blend sugars, butter, egg whites and vanilla until fluffy, stopping once to scrape down sides of work bowl, about 1 minute.
2. Add peanut butter and process until combined, about 20 seconds.
3. Add flour, baking soda and salt and blend until just combined using 2 to 3 on/off turns; do not overprocess.
4. Transfer to a large bowl. Stir in chopped Butterfingers.
5. Mound dough by 1 1/2 tablespoonfuls onto prepared sheets, spacing 2 inches apart. Flatten slightly using fingers.
6. Bake until dry in appearance and centers are still slightly soft to touch, about 15 minutes.
7. Cool on cookie sheets for 3 minutes. Transfer to a rack and cool completely.
8. Store in an airtight container. (Can be prepared ahead. Store 4 days at room temperature or freeze 3 weeks.)
